architecture.concurrency: multiple workers in flight; single documenter serializing
architecture.workaround: none — the gascity#1893 workaround was removed but the bug re-surfaced via a different path

stalls.title: gh-stats: time-to-merge histogram with json, table, markdown output
stalls.stall_root_cause: kickoff routed the bead to gh-stats/sdlc-discipline.worker by setting BOTH --assignee and gc.routed_to. The pool's EffectiveScaleCheck filters --unassigned, so the bead was invisible to the pool reconciler. Pool stayed at 0/5 for 13 minutes; chain only proceeded once --assignee was cleared manually. The same routing bug then re-surfaced at the worker→reviewer→documenter handoffs.

reviewer.note: session_id matches worker — single-conversation continuation, an artifact of v2.0a's reviewer-as-pool-template config; verify on next replay

lessons_for_v2.0: Pool routing must use gc.routed_to ONLY — never --assignee — at every pool→pool transition
The gascity#1893 workaround removal is correct, but the codebase needs an explicit handoff-convention test that catches --assignee on pool targets in CI
Per-bead worktree pattern (.gc/worktrees/<rig>/sdlc/<bead-id>) is correct and survives the routing fix
Phase-completion metadata writes (worker.completed_at, reviewer.completed_at) work correctly in pool agents — the bug is in routing, not lifecycle
